_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 3-5 Sepsis (Revised 05/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured. Applies to patients > 14 years of age.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Treatment Protocol Section 3-5: Sepsis — MDHHS Approved 05/30/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Recognize and treat sepsis early to promote optimal care and survival. Applies to patients > 14 years of age with clinical suspicion of systemic infection who have 2 or more inclusion criteria.

sections.title: Inclusion Criteria
points: Clinical suspicion of systemic infection AND two or more of the following:
(E) SBP < 90 mmHg or evidence of hypoperfusion

sections.title: Treatment
points: Start large bore IV catheter per Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol — start second large bore IV catheter if time permits (Specialist skill)
Place on cardiac monitor and treat rhythm according to appropriate protocol (Specialist skill)
Check blood glucose (may be MFR skill — see Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol)

If the patient meets inclusion criteria: administer NS or LR IV/IO fluid bolus up to 1 liter wide open; reassess patient — repeat boluses to a maximum of 2 L NS or LR as long as vital sign abnormalities persist (Specialist skill)
Monitor for pulmonary edema — if pulmonary edema presents, stop fluids and contact Medical Control for direction
If hypotension persists: refer to Shock-Treatment Protocol
Monitor ETCO2 level (see End Tidal Carbon Dioxide Monitoring-Procedure Protocol) — report levels outside of normal range (35–45 mmHg) to the receiving facility as soon as possible (Specialist skill)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV/IO Fluid Bolus (Sepsis)
key_drugs.dose: Up to 1 liter wide open — repeat boluses to maximum 2 L as long as vital sign abnormalities persist; reassess after each bolus
key_drugs.contraindications: Signs of pulmonary edema — stop fluids and contact Medical Control
key_drugs.notes: Maximum 2 L total. Monitor for pulmonary edema. If hypotension persists after fluid resuscitation, refer to Shock-Treatment Protocol.

deterioration_flags: SBP < 90 mmHg not responding to IV fluid boluses — refer to Shock-Treatment Protocol; contact Medical Control
Signs of pulmonary edema during fluid resuscitation — stop fluids immediately; contact Medical Control for direction
ETCO2 outside 35–45 mmHg — notify receiving facility; may indicate worsening perfusion or ventilatory compromise
Altered mental status with sepsis criteria — airway assessment; expedite transport
scope_notes: Supine positioning, pulse oximetry, temperature assessment: all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
IV access (large bore, two sites if possible): Specialist skill
IV fluid bolus NS or LR: Specialist skill
Dysrhythmia treatment: refer to appropriate cardiac protocol
